title: Object-Oriented Programming: Principles, Patterns, and Practices

topics.title: History of OOP Paradigms: Procedural vs Object-Oriented vs Functional
Objects and Classes: Constructors, Instance Variables, and Class Variables
Access Modifiers and Visibility: Public, Private, Protected, and Package
Interfaces and Abstract Classes: Contracts, Default Methods, and When to Use Each
Single Inheritance: Method Overriding, super Keyword, and Method Resolution
Multiple Inheritance, Interfaces, and Mixins: Diamond Problem and Solutions
Polymorphism: Compile-Time (Overloading) and Runtime (Overriding) Dispatch
Composition Over Inheritance: Has-A vs Is-A and Delegation Pattern
Behavioral Design Patterns: Observer, Strategy, Command, State, and Iterator
Structural Patterns Deep Dive: Decorator, Bridge, Composite, Proxy, and Flyweight
Dependency Injection: IoC Container, Constructor/Setter/Field Injection
DRY, KISS, YAGNI Principles: Code Quality and Design Heuristics
DIP, LSP, and ISP: Dependency Inversion, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation
Exception Handling: Checked/Unchecked Exceptions, Custom Exceptions, and Best Practices
Generics and Templates: Type Bounds, Wildcards, and Type Erasure
Collections Framework: List, Set, Map, Queue Implementations and Big-O Analysis
Concurrency in OOP: Threads, Synchronization, Locks, and Thread-Safe Patterns
Functional Programming in OOP: Lambdas, Streams, and Function Objects
UML Class Diagrams: Notation, Relationships, and Design Documentation
Test-Driven Development: Unit Testing, Mocking, and Test Doubles in OOP
Refactoring: Code Smells, Extract Method, Replace Conditional with Polymorphism
Domain-Driven Design: Aggregates, Value Objects, Repositories, and Bounded Contexts
OOP in Python and C++: Language-Specific Features, Operator Overloading
Placement Guide: OOP Interview Questions, LLD Rounds, and Design Practice
